threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think bus drivers will stop if you wave them down. The locals just nod and keep walking when someone tries that.
Caught a late 333 the other night. Driver knew all the regulars by name, even paused for a guy to finish his takeaway outside the pub. That’s real service.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The way the baker remembers your coffee order and the old man at the corner always shares his stories about the neighborhood's past.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how Kreuzberg’s Späti will sell you a mix of beer and houseplants? Only in Berlin do you leave with a six-pack and a pothos.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local will casually acknowledge the smell of burnt popcorn wafting from the corner store—it's just part of the ambient flavor here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 6-Fulton and a guy serenaded us with an operatic rendition of 'California Dreamin' - honestly made my morning commute feel like a performance art piece.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think they've found the best tapas by Googling. But the real gems are those tiny bars where locals gather, often with no menu at all.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they navigate through the narrow streets, stopping mid-path to snap photos of every old door and balcony.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the bus instead of hopping on a 55. The locals know it’s quicker to walk to the station, especially during the strikes.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week; the driver was singing along to the radio. Made the 3 a.m. trip feel like a mini concert, weirdly uplifting.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a street musician playing Bach on a plastic cello near the U-Bahn, I knew Neukölln had woven itself into my life.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a kebab shop that doubles as an art gallery. Grab a döner while admiring local art—classic Berlin mash-up!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you’ve dodged a startled dog on the tracks at Civic Center, while everyone else just stares at their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading a full Muni bus with his ukulele. Most riders started tapping their feet. Just another day in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí and hip bars, but it’s the local bakeries with fresh ensaïmades that truly make the neighborhood special.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newbies—they’re the ones taking photos of the classic pigeons in Plaça Catalunya like they’ve never seen a bird before.
